name: transportation_workplace_injury_spike_harbor_rig_utl
premise: Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: transportation workplace injury spike harbor rig utl. The moral tension is explicitly framed as rights versus utility: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. Two upstream teams have published contradictory status pages; customers are comparing timestamps. The vendor bridge ends at the top of the hour; after that, configuration changes require a full CAB and you lose the window. Treat unverified telemetry as hypothesis; do not assert facts you cannot support from retained logs. Preserve chain-of-custody for evidence; no bulk deletion or retention-policy changes during the incident.

agents.name: Priya Berg
agents.identity: I am Priya Berg, the accountable decision-maker on this Transportation Technology bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes utility whenever someone invokes rights.

agents.name: Mateo Kim
agents.identity: I am Mateo Kim, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so utility is not sacrificed by default just because rights feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect utility.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to utility to secure rights before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The clock on this bridge is running out. With partial evidence, Priya Berg must decide under rights versus utility. What does Priya Berg do?
